# ZenithNexus - Privacy-First Text to Speech (TTS), Voice Dictation (STT) & Native PDF Reader

ZenithNexus is the ultimate productivity and accessibility browser extension. It allows users to read aloud any web page or PDF file using high-quality local text-to-speech (TTS), and dictate text in any input field using high-accuracy speech-to-text (STT). 

Designed with a strict privacy-first philosophy, ZenithNexus processes all audio synthesis and voice recognition 100% locally on the user's browser (client-side). No audio data or documents are ever sent to the cloud, making it the most secure alternative to cloud-based readers and assistants.

---

## Key Product Offerings

- **Native PDF Reader with Local TTS**: Listen to any local or online PDF file directly in your browser. Includes synchronized word-by-word visual highlighting, adjustable speed (0.5x to 2.0x), and bookmarking. Ideal for multitasking, commuting, or users with dyslexia, ADHD, or visual strain.
- **Private Voice Dictation (STT)**: Dictate emails, forms, and messages instantly using your voice in any text input field. Supports intelligent automatic punctuation in 9 languages, working completely offline. Features an interactive voice commands help guide to inspect all spoken punctuation signs, formatting and text-editing commands in all supported languages.
- **Narrator Mode**: Convert any web article into a clean, podcast-like experience. Automatically strips out ads, navigation menus, and sidebars so you can focus entirely on the content.
- **Absolute Privacy & Offline Capability**: 100% client-side execution. Zero tracking, zero audio logs, and zero cloud data sharing. No account configuration required for core functions.

---

## Technical Architecture & Design

ZenithNexus is structured as a monorepo containing three core packages:
- **`packages/ext`**: The Chrome Extension, compiled using `esbuild` to an IIFE bundle. It utilizes Chrome's storage API and native TTS/STT local APIs to ensure 100% offline functionality.
- **`packages/portal`**: A MERN (MongoDB, Express, React, Node.js) web application. The frontend uses Create React App (CRA) configured with `react-app-rewired` for style-system path mapping. The backend serves client authentication, license keys validation, and account subscription state.
- **`packages/shared`**: Shared styling system (`styled-components` design tokens, light/dark themes, Atoms/Organisms), pricing config (plans, limits), and TypeScript common types.

### Offline & Client-Side Processing Pipeline
1. **Audio Synthesis (TTS)**: ZenithNexus leverages the browser's native Speech Synthesis engine. Voices are accessed offline, utilizing high-quality system voices installed locally on Windows, macOS, ChromeOS, or Linux.
2. **Speech Recognition (STT)**: Employs local Web Speech API recognizers or Web-Assembly local transformer models depending on environment capability, guaranteeing that spoken audio never leaves the machine.
3. **PDF Rendering**: Utilizes `pdfjs-dist` to parse and render documents entirely inside the extension sandbox, completely isolated from network tracking.

---

## Feature Specifications

### Multilingual Support (9 Languages)
The offline voice recognition and automatic punctuation system fully supports:
- Spanish (`es-MX` / `es-ES`)
- English (`en-US` / `en-GB`)
- French (`fr-FR`)
- German (`de-DE`)
- Italian (`it-IT`)
- Portuguese (`pt-BR`)
- Russian (`ru-RU`)
- Japanese (`ja-JP`)
- Chinese (`zh-CN`)

### Keyboard Accessibility Controls
The reader interface supports full keyboard navigation:
- `Space`: Play / Pause narration.
- `Left Arrow`: Rewind 10 seconds.
- `Right Arrow`: Fast-forward 10 seconds.
- `Up Arrow`: Increase speech rate by 0.1x.
- `Down Arrow`: Decrease speech rate by 0.1x.
- `Esc`: Close reader view.

---
